stories.title: AUSL Final Stretch — Utah 14-6, Chicago 13-7, Playing TODAY
stories.angle: Utah is one win away from the bye. Chicago needs to win today to stay alive in this race. The Cascade closed the gap by beating Utah on Wednesday, but Utah still holds the edge — for now.

x_posts.label: Post 1-1
x_posts.text:
The AUSL standings entering today:

Utah Talons — 14-7
Chicago Bandits — 14-7

Cascade run-ruled Utah 10-2 on Wednesday. Bandits beat Blaze 15-7 the same night.

Now they're TIED. And they play today at 4 PM ET on ESPN2.

No. 1 seed = direct bye to the ABC Championship. #AUSL

x_posts.label: Post 1-2
x_posts.text:
AUSL No. 1 seed is on the line RIGHT NOW.

Utah Talons vs. Chicago Bandits — both 14-7. One game separates first place from the play-in path.

4 PM ET on ESPN2. Watch. 🥎 #AUSL #softball

article.headline: Utah Leads Chicago by One Game — and They Play Today

image_concepts.label: Image
image_concepts.raw:
Talons vs. Bandits — No. 1 Seed Showdown

#### Image Concept 1 (Social — 1080x1350)
- **Use:** X post + Facebook image caption (9:00 AM)
- **Status:** not_started
- **Photo search:** "Utah Talons" OR "Chicago Bandits AUSL"
- **Concept:** Graphic with split design — Talons team logo/color on left, Bandits on right. Center text: "No. 1 Seed Showdown / Today 4 PM ET ESPN2" with AUSL brand treatment
- **Overlay text:** "TALONS vs BANDITS / Today 4 PM ET / No. 1 Seed on the Line"

#### Image Concept 2 (Article Hero — 1200x630)
- **Use:** Article 01 featured image
- **Status:** not_started
- **Photo search:** "Jadelyn Allchin Utah Talons" OR "Kat Sandercock Chicago Bandits"
- **Concept:** Action shot of a key Talons or Bandits player in game; clean horizontal crop for article hero

---

research.facts:
AUSL No. 1 Seed Showdown — Talons at Bandits
**Confirmed facts (HIGH confidence — multiple sources):**
- Utah Talons: 14-6 entering July 18 (confirmed from multiple AUSL sources)
- Chicago Bandits: 13-7 entering July 18 (confirmed from multiple AUSL sources)
- Utah leads Chicago by 1 game; a Talons win today = 2-game lead, effectively clinches No. 1 seed
- Utah Talons vs. Chicago Bandits, Saturday July 18, 4 PM ET, ESPN2
- Portland Cascade 10, Utah Talons 2 (run rule, 5 innings) on July 16 at Hillsboro Ballpark — confirmed HIGH (Portland Tribune, Valley Times, multiple sources)
- Chicago Bandits defeated Carolina Blaze 15-7 on July 16
- No. 1 seed earns direct bye to AUSL Championship Series; No. 2/No. 3 must play play-in July 23
- AUSL regular season ends July 20, 2026
- AUSL Championship Series: July 25-27, Davis Diamond, Texas A&M campus, College Station TX
- Play-in game: July 23, Davis Diamond
- Game 1 of Championship on ABC — first professional softball game ever on network television
research.sources:
- Sources: theausl.com; On3 AUSL Week Six Power Rankings; OurSports Central; multiple regional outlets
**MEDIUM confidence:**
- Today's game venue: Bandits' home (prior pipeline noted Talons AT Bandits)
- Bandits starting pitcher and key lineup entries not confirmed for July 18 game
**Context from prior pipeline runs:**
- Talons' postseason clinch: Sharlize Palacios 2-run HR in 7th, Megan Faraimo CG 10K, 5-4 win over OKC on July 16
- Bandits ran off 9-game winning streak (AUSL record) before Portland snapped it
- Key Bandits players: Lexi Kilfoyl, Kat Sandercock (pitching), Sami Williams (offense)
**CORRECTION TO PRIOR PIPELINE:**
- July 17 pipeline stated "Utah 14-6, Chicago 14-7" — the Chicago record was incorrect (should be 13-7). Utah record of 14-6 is confirmed correct.

stories.title: Volts Top Cascade 7-2 — Play-In Fate Locked In for Portland
stories.angle: The Cascade is in the playoffs. But after Friday's loss, their path runs through a win-or-go-home play-in. What happened in the 7-2 loss, and how do they respond when the Volts come back tonight?

x_posts.label: Post 2
x_posts.text:
Texas Volts 7, Portland Cascade 2 — Friday night.

The team outside the playoff picture just beat the team headed to the postseason.

Cascade drops to 12-10. Play-in game July 23 at Davis Diamond is locked in. #AUSL #softball

article.headline: Volts Beat Cascade 7-2. Portland's Path to Davis Diamond Now Runs Through a Play-In.

image_concepts.label: Image
image_concepts.raw:
Volts Beat Cascade 7-2 — Play-In Fate Sealed

#### Image Concept 3 (Social — 1080x1350)
- **Use:** X post + Facebook image caption (11:00 AM)
- **Status:** not_started
- **Photo search:** "NiJaree Canady Texas Volts" OR "Megan Grant Portland Cascade"
- **Concept:** Action shot of either NiJaree Canady (Volts) or Megan Grant (Cascade) in game action; text overlay with game result "VOLTS 7 / CASCADE 2" and "July 17, 2026"

#### Image Concept 4 (Article Hero — 1200x630)
- **Use:** Article 02 featured image
- **Status:** not_started
- **Photo search:** "NiJaree Canady Texas Tech" OR "NiJaree Canady AUSL"
- **Concept:** Close portrait or action of Canady in pitching motion; horizontal crop for article hero

---

research.facts:
Volts Beat Cascade 7-2 — Playoff Picture Shaken
**Confirmed facts (HIGH confidence — multiple sources):**
- Texas Volts defeated Portland Cascade 7-2 on Friday, July 17, 2026
- Final line: Volts 7 runs, 10 hits, 1 error; Cascade 2 runs, 4 hits, 0 errors
research.sources:
- Source: theausl.com standings/schedule data; albatrossports.com AUSL tracker
- Portland Cascade drops to approximately 11-10 after this loss
- Cascade still holds No. 3 seed (Talons, Bandits, Cascade are top 3 teams per confirmed standings)
- Texas Volts had been outside playoff picture all season
**HIGH confidence (from prior pipeline):**
- NiJaree Canady: No. 2 overall pick in 2026 AUSL College Draft
- Canady record entering July 17: 0-3, 6.87 ERA (17 ER, 7 HR allowed)
- Mechanical issue: shifted to "open" stance post-injury; former Stanford coach Tori Nyberg assisting
- Canady did earn first AUSL win July 10 (complete game, 6 K)
**MEDIUM confidence:**
- Whether Canady pitched in the July 17 game (not confirmed from available sources)
- Canady's updated record/ERA post-July 17 game
**LOW — do not use:**
- Specific pitching lines from July 17 game without source confirmation

stories.title: PGF Nationals Opens Tomorrow — 1 Day Out
stories.angle: The tournament is here. One day out, rosters locked, ESPN broadcasting the championships. The summer's biggest grassroots moment begins tomorrow.

x_posts.label: Post 3
x_posts.text:
PGF Nationals opens TOMORROW at Great Park in Irvine, CA.

72 teams. 22 states. 18U and 16U Premier divisions.

Sept. 1 recruiting window = 44 days. Every at-bat counts.

Championships on ESPNU and ESPN+. Don't sleep on this weekend. #PGFNationals #softball

article.headline: 72 Teams. 22 States. The PGF Nationals Open Tomorrow.

image_concepts.label: Image
image_concepts.raw:
PGF Nationals Tomorrow

#### Image Concept 5 (Social — 1080x1350)
- **Use:** X post + Facebook image caption (1:00 PM)
- **Status:** not_started
- **Photo search:** "Great Park Irvine softball" (stock alternative if imagn not available)
- **Concept:** Aerial or wide shot of Great Park Irvine fields; text overlay: "PGF Nationals / Starts TOMORROW / Great Park, Irvine CA"

#### Image Concept 6 (Article Hero — 1200x630)
- **Use:** Article 03 featured image
- **Status:** not_started
- **Photo search:** "PGF softball Nationals" OR use generic travel softball game action
- **Concept:** Wide game action shot of 18U or 16U softball game; horizontal for article hero

---

research.facts:
PGF Nationals Opens Tomorrow
**Confirmed facts (HIGH confidence):**
- PGF National Championships: July 19–August 4, 2026
- Location: Great Park, Irvine, California (primary venue; also Bill Barber Park, Fountain Valley Sports Park, Harvard Park, Huntington Beach Sports Complex)
- Week 1: 18U and 16U divisions, July 19-25
- Week 2: 14U, 12U, 10U divisions, July 26–August 2
- Roster freeze occurred July 13 at 11:59 PM PST
research.sources:
- Source: pgfnationals.com; premiergirlsfastpitch.com; loscerritosnews.net (July 16, 2026)
**HIGH confidence:**
- September 1 = Class of 2028 college recruiting contact window opens (44 days from July 18)
- Tournament is the largest girls' fastpitch event in the United States
**MEDIUM confidence:**
- Top 18U contenders: Arizona Storm-Mendez, USA Premier National, WA Ladyhawks, Southern California Sting, Athletics Mercado (from Los Cerritos Community News July 16 preview)
- Arizona Storm Appel also mentioned as top 18U contender in prior pipeline runs
**Context from prior pipeline:**
- PGF Nationals rosters locked July 13; prior pipeline tracked Arizona Storm Appel as top 18U contender

stories.title: Cascade Locked In — Megan Grant and the Play-In Path to Davis Diamond
stories.angle: She came to the pros to win. Now the Cascade has to win one play-in game to keep her season alive. The pressure lands squarely on the team's best bat.

x_posts.label: Post 4
x_posts.text:
Megan Grant hit 42 HRs to break a 31-year NCAA record. She leads the AUSL in home runs.

Now her season comes down to one play-in game on July 23 at Davis Diamond.

Win = Championship. Lose = done.

This is what the pro game looks like. #AUSL #softball

article.headline: Megan Grant Came to the Pros to Win. Now She Has to Do It Through a Play-In.

image_concepts.label: Image
image_concepts.raw:
Megan Grant / Cascade Play-In Path

#### Image Concept 7 (Social — 1080x1350)
- **Use:** X post + Facebook image caption (3:00 PM)
- **Status:** not_started
- **Photo search:** "Megan Grant Portland Cascade" OR "Megan Grant UCLA"
- **Concept:** Action shot of Grant at bat or in field; text overlay: "MEGAN GRANT / The Play-In / July 23 · Davis Diamond"

---

research.facts:
Cascade's Play-In Path
**Confirmed facts (HIGH confidence):**
- Portland Cascade lost 7-2 to Texas Volts on July 17, 2026 (confirmed above)
- Cascade now approximately 11-10 with 2 games left
- Cascade locked into No. 3 seed barring dramatic swing in final 2 games
- No. 3 seed plays single-elimination play-in July 23 at Davis Diamond
- Play-in winner advances to Championship Series July 25-27
**HIGH confidence:**
- Megan Grant: 42 HR NCAA record at UCLA (broke Laura Espinoza's 37 HR from 1995)
- Grant leads AUSL in home runs (from prior pipeline, confirmed multiple sources)
- Grant's 313-ft debut HR was AUSL record
**MEDIUM confidence:**
- Cascade's next opponent and game schedule for final 2 games (July 19-20)
- Cascade pitching rotation heading into play-in

stories.title: 7 Days to the AUSL Championship — Your Guide to Davis Diamond and the ABC Debut
stories.angle: For readers finding professional softball for the first time through the ABC announcement, this is the guide: what's happening, where it is, and why it matters.

x_posts.label: Post 5
x_posts.text:
One week from today: the AUSL Championship at Davis Diamond, Texas A&M.

Game 1 is on ABC — the first pro softball game ever on network television.

No. 1 seed: direct to Championship. No. 2 vs No. 3: play-in first (July 23).

History is coming. #AUSL #softball

article.headline: One Week to the AUSL Championship. Here's Your Guide to Davis Diamond and the ABC Debut.

research.facts:
AUSL Championship — Davis Diamond Context
**Confirmed facts (HIGH confidence):**
- AUSL Championship Series: July 25-27, 2026, Davis Diamond, Texas A&M, College Station, TX
- Play-in game: July 23, 2026, Davis Diamond
- Game 1 on ABC — first professional softball game ever broadcast on network television
- Best-of-three Championship Series format
research.sources:
- Source: theausl.com/championship; multiple pipeline confirmation from official AUSL announcement
**MEDIUM confidence:**
- Davis Diamond capacity (approximately 5,000 fans; listed as "premier softball facility" in multiple sources)
- Davis Diamond built in 2024 on Texas A&M campus
